title: Commission approves €402 million Spanish State aid for road transport companies facing increased fuel prices
content:
The European Commission has approved a €402 million Spanish State aid scheme to support road
transport companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €402 million Spanish State aid scheme to support road transport companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.

title: Commission presents Council with ambitious Indonesia agreements
content: Today the European Commission presented its proposals on the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) and the Investment Protection Agreement (IPA) with Indonesia to the Council for their signature and conclusion.

title: High Representative Kallas with Commissioners Kos and Brunner in Türkiye to advance bilateral cooperation
content: Today and tomorrow, High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy and Vice-President of the European Commission, Kaja Kallas, Commissioner for Enlargement, Marta Kos, and Commissioner for Internal Affairs and Migration, Magnus Brunner, will travel to Türkiye...

title: Lithuania receives first €956.3 million payment under SAFE
content: On 24 June 2026, Lithuania received its first payment of € 956.3 million under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) defence financing instrument, representing 15% of its total allocation of €6.4 billion.
snippet: On 24 June 2026, Lithuania received its first payment of € 956.3 million under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) defence financing instrument, representing 15% of its total allocation of €6.4

title: What the latest data reveals about labour market imbalances across Europe
content: Europe faces a massive labour mismatch. To bridge the gap between shortages and surpluses, we need smarter reforms and mobility.
snippet: Europe faces a massive labour mismatch. To bridge the gap between shortages and surpluses, we need smarter reforms and mobility.

title: Your Voice on EU Enlargement: call for organisations to host outreach and engagement events
content: The European Commission has launched “Your Voice on EU Enlargement”, a call for applications to organize outreach and engagement events on EU enlargement in EU Member States.
snippet: The European Commission has launched “Your Voice on EU Enlargement”, a call for applications to organize outreach and engagement events on EU enlargement in EU Member States.

title: CEF Energy: 6th call for Cross-Border Renewable Energy Projects to obtain the status launched
content: On 29 June, CINEA launched the 6th call for projects aiming to obtain the Cross-Border Renewable Energy (CB RES) status, the last status call under the current 2021–2027 Multiannual Financial Framework (MFF).
snippet: On 29 June, CINEA launched the 6th call for projects aiming to obtain the Cross-Border Renewable Energy (CB RES) status, the last status call under the current 2021–2027 Multiannual Financial

title: Adoption of the reform of the Research Fund for Coal and Steel
content: Today, the Council of the EU adopted two Decisions reforming the Research Fund for Coal and Steel.
snippet: Today, the Council of the EU adopted two Decisions reforming the Research Fund for Coal and Steel.

title: Eurostat: Renewable energy and natural gas supply up in 2025
content: In 2025, according to preliminary data, the supply of natural gas and renewable energy in the EU grew compared with 2024, while the supply of coal and petroleum products decreased.
snippet: In 2025, according to preliminary data, the supply of natural gas and renewable energy in the EU grew compared with 2024, while the supply of coal and petroleum products decreased.

title: Commission calls on the Netherlands to improve competition for international rail passenger services
content: The European Commission has informed the Netherlands that the rules for the allocation of capacity on the Dutch main railway network may be in breach of EU antitrust rules.
snippet: The European Commission has informed the Netherlands that the rules for the allocation of capacity on the Dutch main railway network may be in breach of EU antitrust rules.

title: Ensuring fairness and safety: €3 customs duty for low-value parcels
content: From 1 July 2026, the EU will introduce a temporary €3 customs duty on low-value parcels imported from outside the EU, mainly through e-commerce.
snippet: From 1 July 2026, the EU will introduce a temporary €3 customs duty on low-value parcels imported from outside the EU, mainly through e-commerce.

title: Casting his own line: A young fisher’s path to independence in Réunion
content: At just 27, Richard Delmas is charting his own course in the waters off Réunion Island. Born into a fishing family, he grew up at sea. Yet he chose not to pursue his father’s large-scale fishing operations. Instead, with support from the EU, he launched a small one-person fishing business
snippet: At just 27, Richard Delmas is charting his own course in the waters off Réunion Island. Born into a fishing family, he grew up at sea. Yet he chose not to pursue his father’s large-scale fishing

title: Commission welcomes political agreement on criminal law rules to fight child sexual abuse
content: On 22 June, the European Parliament and Council reached a political agreement on the Directive updating the criminal law rules on child sexual abuse and sexual exploitation, a major milestone in the protection of children as worrying new developments make the agreement all the more timely.
snippet: On 22 June, the European Parliament and Council reached a political agreement on the Directive updating the criminal law rules on child sexual abuse and sexual exploitation, a major milestone in the

title: Better enforcement of passenger rights: new EU agreement strengthens protection across all modes of transport
content: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached by the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on improved enforcement of passenger rights across all modes of transport.
snippet: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached by the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on improved enforcement of passenger rights across all modes of transport.

title: The Commission takes stock of situation on oil markets amid developments in the Middle East
content: In a meeting of the Oil Coordination Group held today, the Commission and EU countries discussed the security of oil supply situation in the EU in light of recent reports on ongoing negotiations in the Middle East.
snippet: In a meeting of the Oil Coordination Group held today, the Commission and EU countries discussed the security of oil supply situation in the EU in light of recent reports on ongoing negotiations in

title: Commission proposes to extend temporary protection of people fleeing Ukraine for an additional year
content: On 26 June, the Commission proposed to extend the temporary protection for people fleeing Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine until 4 March 2028, providing continued legal certainty, stability and predictability to almost 4.4 million people displaced from Ukraine currently living in the EU.
snippet: On 26 June, the Commission proposed to extend the temporary protection for people fleeing Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine until 4 March 2028, providing continued legal certainty, stability

title: Reality Check for the Evaluation of the Firearms Directive
content: This Reality Check meeting marks the first large-scale, direct dialogue between DG HOME and the private firearms sector. Participants included European associations representing firearms and ammunition manufacturers, firearms traders, sport shooters, hunters, and firearms collectors.
snippet: This Reality Check meeting marks the first large-scale, direct dialogue between DG HOME and the private firearms sector. Participants included European associations representing firearms and

title: Commissioner Síkela concludes Global Gateway investment mission to Brazil
content: Commissioner for International Partnerships Jozef Síkela’s mission to Brazil was about strengthening the EU-Brazil partnership through Global Gateway, the EU’s strategy for investing in joint projects in critical raw materials, sustainable transport, digital technology and clean energy.
snippet: Commissioner for International Partnerships Jozef Síkela’s mission to Brazil was about strengthening the EU-Brazil partnership through Global Gateway, the EU’s strategy for investing in joint projects

title: Projects to Policy Seminar: security research projects feed into policy making
content: The annual Projects to Policy Seminar (PPS) took place on 10 and 11 June in Brussels to gather all the newly begun research projects in the area of civil security for society. This year was a particularly busy edition with over 160 participants drawn from almost 40 new projects.
snippet: The annual Projects to Policy Seminar (PPS) took place on 10 and 11 June in Brussels to gather all the newly begun research projects in the area of civil security for society. This year was a

title: Why Mobility Matters for Europe's Competitiveness
content: From apprentices to entrepreneurs, discover how mobility programmes help build skills, unlock opportunities and strengthen Europe's competitiveness. Join the discussion on 1 July in Brussels or online.

title: Make your voice heard: Join the EU Youth Policy Dialogue on COP31
content: The European Commission and the European Economic and Social Committee, in cooperation with the European Youth Forum and Generation Climate Europe, will organise the EU Youth Policy Dialogue on COP31 on Thursday 1 October 2026.
snippet: The European Commission and the European Economic and Social Committee, in cooperation with the European Youth Forum and Generation Climate Europe, will organise the EU Youth Policy Dialogue on COP31

title: EU agri-food trade surplus holds firm in the first 4 months of the year
content: The latest monthly agri-food trade report shows developments in imports and exports of EU agri-food products in April 2026.
snippet: The latest monthly agri-food trade report shows developments in imports and exports of EU agri-food products in April 2026.

title: Commission opens investigation into possible anticompetitive conduct by Sanofi regarding the promotion of a flu vaccine for vulnerable patients

title: Fifth Edition of “The Hub. Insights”
content: The central challenge for PCVE is increasingly crisis anticipation, rather than crisis management. This issue aims to support that task by mapping emerging and highly salient trends for PCVE practitioners, policy makers, and researchers.
snippet: The central challenge for PCVE is increasingly crisis anticipation, rather than crisis management. This issue aims to support that task by mapping emerging and highly salient trends for PCVE

title: #EUBeachCleanup campaign launch
content: Cyprus, Commissioner Costas Kadis has officially launched this year’s edition of the #EUBeachCleanup campaign.
snippet: Cyprus, Commissioner Costas Kadis has officially launched this year’s edition of the #EUBeachCleanup campaign.

title: First-ever EU tripartite agreement signed to boost energy storage
content: The European Commission has rallied a number of EU energy ministers, storage developers and manufacturers, renewable energy developers, energy-consuming industries and financial institutions around the first-ever EU tripartite agreement on energy storage.

title: Zagreb Hosts the Final SUNSHINE Project Training Seminar on EU Space for Disaster Resilience
content: From 23 to 26 June 2026, the EU-funded SUNSHINE project held its fourth and final Training Seminar in Zagreb, Croatia.
snippet: From 23 to 26 June 2026, the EU-funded SUNSHINE project held its fourth and final Training Seminar in Zagreb, Croatia.
